message:
Add raw_message parameter to read_syslog operator

The read_syslog operator now supports an optional raw_message parameter
that stores the original, unparsed syslog message in a user-specified
field. This allows users to preserve the complete input when parsing
syslog data for debugging, audit, or retention purposes.

The feature works with both RFC 5424 and RFC 3164 (legacy) formats,
and correctly handles multiline syslog messages by concatenating all
lines into the raw message field.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+// Test raw_message with multiline messages
+from {x: "<165>1 2023-01-01T00:00:00Z host app 123 - - Line1\nLine2\nLine3"}
+write_lines
+read_syslog raw_message=raw
+select message, raw

@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+// Test raw_message with nested field path
+from {x: r#"<165>1 2023-01-01T00:00:00Z host app 123 - - Test"#}
+write_lines
+read_syslog raw_message=original.line
+select message, original

@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+// Test raw_message with RFC 3164 (legacy) format
+from {x: "<34>Oct 11 22:14:15 mymachine su: failed login"}
+write_lines
+read_syslog raw_message=raw
+select content, raw

@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+// Test raw_message with RFC 5424 format
+from {x: r#"<165>1 2023-01-01T00:00:00Z host app 123 - - Test message"#}
+write_lines
+read_syslog raw_message=raw
+select message, raw
